title: Completed repo commands can refresh the wrong repository after a repo switch
reasoning: Both command and triage mutations run against the repo passed in their mutation variables, but their success handlers invalidate queries using the currently selected repo from component state. If a user starts a command for repo A and switches to repo B before it finishes, the mutation result is logged but invalidation targets repo B instead of the repo whose data actually changed. Repo A's findings, feature map, and diff can remain stale until another refresh happens.
recommendation: Use the mutation variables passed to onSuccess to invalidate the repo that the mutation actually ran against, for example onSuccess: (result, variables) => invalidateRepo(queryClient, variables.repo.id). Apply this to both commandMutation and triageMutation.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests were provided for renderer mutation behavior, and this race requires simulating a repo selection change while an async mutation is in flight.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a ClawpatchApp test with two repos where window.clawpatch.commands.run is deferred, start a command on repo A, select repo B, resolve the command, and assert invalidation/refetch happens for repo A rather than repo B.
minimumFixScope: Update ClawpatchApp mutation success handlers to use mutation variables for cache invalidation.
